questions.question: Which of the following describes a 'Sealed Class' introduced in Java 17?
options: A class that cannot be instantiated.
A class that cannot be inherited by any other class.
A class that restricts which other classes may extend or implement it.
A class that can only be used within the same package.
questions.correctAnswer: A class that restricts which other classes may extend or implement it.
questions.explanation: Sealed classes and interfaces restrict which other classes or interfaces may extend or implement them.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'permits' keyword in a sealed class declaration?
options: To allow a class to access private members of another class.
To list the specific subclasses allowed to extend the sealed class.
To grant permission for a method to be overridden.
To define which packages can use the class.
questions.correctAnswer: To list the specific subclasses allowed to extend the sealed class.
questions.explanation: The 'permits' clause specifies the classes that are allowed to extend a sealed class.

questions.question: Which Collector is used to group elements of a stream into a Map based on a classification function?
options: Collectors.toMap()
Collectors.partitioningBy()
Collectors.groupingBy()
Collectors.joining()
questions.correctAnswer: Collectors.groupingBy()
questions.explanation: Collectors.groupingBy() groups stream elements into a Map according to a classifier.

questions.question: In Java 8+, what is the difference between 'map' and 'flatMap' in the Stream API?
options: map is for objects, flatMap is for primitives.
map transforms each element into another element, flatMap transforms each element into a stream and flattens them.
map is sequential, flatMap is parallel.
There is no difference; they are aliases.
questions.correctAnswer: map transforms each element into another element, flatMap transforms each element into a stream and flattens them.
questions.explanation: flatMap is used when each element of a stream can produce multiple elements (a stream itself) that need to be merged into a single outcome stream.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about Virtual Threads (introduced in Java 21)?
options: They are managed by the Operating System.
They are lightweight threads managed by the Java Virtual Machine.
They can only be used for CPU-intensive tasks.
They replace platform threads entirely.
questions.correctAnswer: They are lightweight threads managed by the Java Virtual Machine.
questions.explanation: Virtual threads are lightweight threads that reduce the effort of writing, maintaining, and observing high-throughput concurrent applications.

questions.question: What does 'type erasure' mean in Java Generics?
options: The removal of type parameters at compile time to ensure backward compatibility with older Java versions.
The ability to change the type of a variable at runtime.
The process of converting a primitive type to its wrapper class.
The error that occurs when a generic type is used incorrectly.
questions.correctAnswer: The removal of type parameters at compile time to ensure backward compatibility with older Java versions.
questions.explanation: Java compiler uses type erasure to replace all type parameters in generic types with their bounds or Object if the type parameters are unbounded.

questions.question: What is the result of using 'var' for a variable that is initialized with a null literal?
options: The variable is of type Object.
The variable is of type Null.
A compilation error occurs.
The variable type is inferred at runtime.
questions.correctAnswer: A compilation error occurs.
questions.explanation: The 'var' keyword requires an initializer from which the type can be inferred; 'null' alone does not provide enough type information.

questions.question: Which interface should be implemented to use a class in a 'try-with-resources' statement?
options: java.io.Serializable
java.lang.Cloneable
java.lang.AutoCloseable
java.lang.Runnable
questions.correctAnswer: java.lang.AutoCloseable
questions.explanation: Objects that implement AutoCloseable (or its sub-interface Closeable) can be used as resources in try-with-resources.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Optional.ifPresentOrElse()' introduced in Java 9?
options: To check if a value is present and return a boolean.
To perform an action if a value is present, or a different action if it is not.
To provide a default value if the Optional is empty.
To throw an exception if the Optional is empty.
questions.correctAnswer: To perform an action if a value is present, or a different action if it is not.
questions.explanation: ifPresentOrElse(Consumer, Runnable) allows executing a consumer if present, otherwise executing a runnable.

questions.question: Which memory area in the JVM was replaced by 'Metaspace' in Java 8?
options: Heap Memory
Stack Memory
Permanent Generation (PermGen)
Code Cache
questions.correctAnswer: Permanent Generation (PermGen)
questions.explanation: Starting with Java 8, PermGen was removed and replaced by Metaspace, which is part of native memory.

questions.question: What is a 'record' in Java (introduced as a standard feature in Java 16)?
options: A new way to define interfaces.
A special type of class that acts as a transparent carrier for immutable data.
A database connection object.
A log file entry in the JVM.
questions.correctAnswer: A special type of class that acts as a transparent carrier for immutable data.
questions.explanation: Records are classes that automatically generate constructors, accessors, equals(), hashCode(), and toString() for a set of fields.

questions.question: Which of these is a functional interface in the java.util.function package that takes one argument and returns a boolean?
options: Consumer
Supplier
Predicate
Function
questions.correctAnswer: Predicate
questions.explanation: Predicate<T> takes one argument of type T and returns a boolean value.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of the G1 (Garbage First) collector?
options: It is the fastest collector for small heaps.
It provides predictable pause times for large heaps.
It does not require any CPU overhead.
It only collects garbage from the young generation.
questions.correctAnswer: It provides predictable pause times for large heaps.
questions.explanation: G1 is designed for multi-processor machines with large memory, aiming for low pause times with high probability.

questions.question: How can you prevent a field from being serialized in Java?
options: By marking it 'static'.
By marking it 'volatile'.
By marking it 'transient'.
By marking it 'final'.
questions.correctAnswer: By marking it 'transient'.
questions.explanation: The transient keyword indicates that a field should not be part of the object's serialized form.

questions.question: What is 'Pattern Matching for instanceof' (introduced in Java 16)?
options: A way to compare strings using regex.
A feature that allows declaring a variable directly in the instanceof check.
A new way to implement the Factory pattern.
A mechanism for matching file patterns in the IO package.
questions.correctAnswer: A feature that allows declaring a variable directly in the instanceof check.
questions.explanation: It simplifies the use of instanceof by allowing a pattern variable to be declared and used if the check is true (e.g., if (obj instanceof String s)).

questions.question: What does 'CompletableFuture.supplyAsync()' do?
options: Executes a task on the main thread.
Returns a new CompletableFuture that is asynchronously completed by a task running in the ForkJoinPool.commonPool().
Waits for a task to complete and returns the result.
Synchronizes two different threads.
questions.correctAnswer: Returns a new CompletableFuture that is asynchronously completed by a task running in the ForkJoinPool.commonPool().
questions.explanation: supplyAsync runs a task asynchronously and returns a CompletableFuture containing the result of the supplier.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about 'default' methods in interfaces?
options: They cannot have a body.
They make the interface a class.
They allow adding new methods to interfaces without breaking existing implementations.
They must be overridden by every implementing class.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow adding new methods to interfaces without breaking existing implementations.
questions.explanation: Default methods provide a default implementation and don't force implementing classes to override them.

questions.question: What is the 'String.intern()' method used for?
options: To convert a string to an integer.
To store string literals in a global pool and return a reference to the pooled instance.
To find the length of a string.
To reverse a string.
questions.correctAnswer: To store string literals in a global pool and return a reference to the pooled instance.
questions.explanation: intern() ensures that all identical strings share the same memory in the String Constant Pool.

questions.question: Which tool is used to monitor and profile Java applications with very low overhead (part of the JDK)?
options: JConsole
VisualVM
Java Flight Recorder (JFR)
JDB
questions.correctAnswer: Java Flight Recorder (JFR)
questions.explanation: JFR is a profiling and event-gathering framework built into the JVM with near-zero overhead.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'java.lang.invoke.MethodHandles' API?
options: To handle exceptions in a new way.
To provide a modernized, faster alternative to the Core Reflection API.
To manage network sockets.
To create graphical user interfaces.
questions.correctAnswer: To provide a modernized, faster alternative to the Core Reflection API.
questions.explanation: MethodHandles provide a typesafe, low-level way to find and invoke methods, often faster than reflection.

questions.question: What happens when you call 'Thread.start()' twice on the same thread object?
options: The thread restarts from the beginning.
The second call is ignored.
An 'IllegalThreadStateException' is thrown.
The thread runs concurrently with itself.
questions.correctAnswer: An 'IllegalThreadStateException' is thrown.
questions.explanation: A thread cannot be started more than once. Attempting to do so results in an IllegalThreadStateException.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Java Module System (Project Jigsaw)?
options: Strong encapsulation.
Explicit dependencies (requires).
Automatic memory management (Garbage Collection).
Reduced footprint through jlink.
questions.correctAnswer: Automatic memory management (Garbage Collection).
questions.explanation: GC is a feature of the JVM, not specifically a feature of the Module System introduced in Java 9.

questions.question: What is the result of 'Stream.of(1, 2, 3).reduce(0, (a, b) -> a + b)'?
options: Compilation error
questions.explanation: The reduce operation sums the elements: 0 + 1 = 1, 1 + 2 = 3, 3 + 3 = 6.

questions.question: Which annotation is used to indicate that a method is intended to override a method in a superclass?
options: @Inherited
@Deprecated
@Override
@SuppressWarnings
questions.correctAnswer: @Override
questions.explanation: The @Override annotation tells the compiler that the method is meant to override a superclass method, providing a compile-time check.

questions.question: In Java Generics, what does '<? extends T>' represent?
options: A wildcard that matches any class that is a superclass of T.
A wildcard that matches T or any subclass of T.
A wildcard that matches only T.
A wildcard that matches any class.
questions.correctAnswer: A wildcard that matches T or any subclass of T.
questions.explanation: This is called an upper-bounded wildcard, restricting the unknown type to be T or a subtype of T.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'var handles' (java.lang.invoke.VarHandle) introduced in Java 9?
options: To define variable-length arrays.
To provide a standard way to invoke the equivalents of java.util.concurrent.atomic and sun.misc.Unsafe operations.
To handle 'var' keyword inference errors.
To manage environment variables.
questions.correctAnswer: To provide a standard way to invoke the equivalents of java.util.concurrent.atomic and sun.misc.Unsafe operations.
questions.explanation: VarHandles provide fence operations and atomic access to fields/array elements with finer control than volatile.

questions.question: Which of the following classes is NOT part of the Java Collections Framework?
options: HashMap
ArrayList
String
LinkedList
questions.correctAnswer: String
questions.explanation: String is a class for character sequences, not a collection container.

questions.question: What is the default value of a 'float' variable in Java when declared as a class member?
options: null
NaN
questions.explanation: Instance variables of type float are initialized to 0.0f by default.

questions.question: Which method of the 'Object' class is used to notify a single thread waiting on an object's monitor?
options: notifyAll()
notify()
signal()
wakeUp()
questions.correctAnswer: notify()
questions.explanation: The notify() method wakes up a single thread that is waiting on this object's monitor.

questions.question: What is 'Text Blocks' (introduced in Java 15)?
options: A way to define large chunks of text using triple quotes ( """ ).
A new GUI component for displaying text.
A mechanism for blocking certain words in strings.
A type of encrypted data block.
questions.correctAnswer: A way to define large chunks of text using triple quotes ( """ ).
questions.explanation: Text Blocks allow multi-line string literals without the need for most escape sequences, improving readability.
